product_overview: Sharetopus is a web app that lets creators and small teams create content once and publish or schedule it across multiple social platforms, manage connected accounts, and handle subscription billing. The product combines a public marketing site with an authenticated dashboard for scheduling, account connections, and history/analytics.
core_goals: Allow users to create, schedule, and batch-publish posts (text, image, video) across multiple social platforms from one composer.
Provide OAuth-based connected account management with platform-specific options and token lifecycle handling.
Gate protected features behind subscription plans and integrate Stripe for checkout and billing management.
Offer a clear marketing surface (landing + pricing) to convert visitors into subscribing users.
Expose a searchable history of published content and basic per-post analytics for troubleshooting and insights.

features.name: Marketing Website
features.description: Public marketing site with landing, pricing, and legal pages to communicate product value and convert visitors to signups.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Scroll landing page sections -> Click call-to-action button for signup -> Navigate to /login -> See authentication entry
Navigate to / -> Click link to pricing -> Navigate to /pricing -> Read plan comparison and pricing tiers
Navigate to /pricing -> Click 'Start free trial' CTA -> Navigate to /login -> See sign-in or sign-up options
Navigate to / -> Click Privacy Policy link -> Open privacy policy content -> Read privacy policy
Navigate to / -> Click Terms of Service link -> Open terms of service content -> Read terms of service

features.name: Authentication with Clerk
features.description: Sign in and sign up flows using Clerk-hosted UI, with support for email/password and OAuth providers and middleware protection for authenticated routes.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Enter email and password -> Click Sign in -> See successful sign-in and redirect to /dashboard
Navigate to /login -> Click OAuth provider button -> Complete OAuth provider login -> Be redirected and signed in to /dashboard
Navigate to /login -> Enter invalid password -> Click Sign in -> See authentication error message and remain on /login
While signed in on /dashboard -> Click Log out -> See sign-out and redirect to /

features.name: Connected Social Accounts Management
features.description: Connect and manage social accounts (Pinterest, LinkedIn) via OAuth, view account metadata, and disconnect accounts as needed.
user_flows: Navigate to /accounts -> Open connected accounts page -> Click Connect Pinterest -> Start OAuth connection flow for Pinterest -> Complete OAuth provider login -> Be redirected to /accounts -> See new Pinterest account listed

Navigate to /accounts -> Open connected accounts page -> Click Connect LinkedIn -> Start OAuth connection flow for LinkedIn -> Complete OAuth provider login -> Be redirected to /accounts -> See new LinkedIn account listed
Navigate to /accounts -> Open connected accounts page -> Click Disconnect on an existing account -> Confirm disconnect -> See account removed from list

Navigate to /accounts -> Click Connect Pinterest -> Start OAuth connection flow for Pinterest -> Deny permissions on provider screen -> Be redirected to /accounts -> See connection error notification and no new account listed
Navigate to /accounts -> Open connected accounts page -> Attempt to connect while at account limit for tier -> See subscription gating message with link to /pricing or /settings

features.name: Subscription and Billing with Stripe
features.description: Manage subscription tiers and billing through Stripe Checkout and the Stripe Customer Portal from the app settings.
user_flows: Navigate to /settings -> Open billing settings -> Click upgrade or change plan button -> Start Stripe Checkout hosted flow -> Complete payment in Stripe Checkout -> Be redirected to /settings -> See active subscription status

Navigate to /settings -> Open billing settings -> Click 'Open Customer Portal' -> Be redirected to Stripe Customer Portal -> Update payment method or subscription -> Return to /settings and see updated subscription details

Navigate to /settings -> Open billing settings -> Click upgrade or change plan button -> Start Stripe Checkout -> Enter invalid card details -> See payment error from Stripe and remain on checkout or receive error notification in app
Navigate to /settings -> Open billing settings -> Click upgrade but network/API fails -> See error notification and remain on billing settings

features.name: Post Creation and Scheduling
features.description: Create, customize, batch, schedule, edit, and cancel posts (text, image, video) targeting one or more connected accounts with per-platform options and media validation.
user_flows: Navigate to /scheduled -> Open scheduled posts page -> Click New Post or open composer -> Select target social accounts -> Enter post text content -> Upload image or video media -> Choose future date and time -> Click Schedule -> See scheduled post listed in /scheduled
Navigate to /scheduled -> Open scheduled posts page -> Click on an existing scheduled post -> Edit post text and scheduled date -> Click Save -> See updated scheduled post details
Navigate to /scheduled -> Open scheduled posts page -> Click Cancel on a scheduled post -> Confirm cancel -> See post removed or marked cancelled in list

Navigate to /scheduled -> Open scheduled posts page -> Click New Post -> Select target accounts but no accounts are connected -> See subscription/account gating or 'no accounts' message with link to /accounts
Navigate to /scheduled -> Open composer -> Upload unsupported file type or file exceeding size limit -> See media validation error and reject upload
Navigate to /scheduled -> Open composer -> Fill post and schedule -> Click Schedule -> Backend returns an error when posting -> See scheduling failure notification and post remains unscheduled

features.name: Content History
features.description: View published posts, grouped by batch with status and basic performance metadata, and filter or inspect post details for troubleshooting.
user_flows: Navigate to /history -> Open history page -> See list of past posts grouped by batch -> Click a post to view details -> See status, platform, media and metadata
Navigate to /history -> Open history page -> Use filter or search to narrow posts -> See filtered result set
Navigate to /history -> Open history page -> Click a failed post -> See failure reason and error message -> Use provided troubleshooting guidance
Navigate to /history -> Open history page -> API returns an error or times out -> See error message and option to retry or refresh

routes.description: Authentication entry using Clerk for sign in

known_limitations.issue: Some API endpoint paths in this summary are inferred and may not exactly match implementation
known_limitations.impact: Tests relying on inferred endpoints may require adjustment to match real routes
